_meta.source: National Building Code of India 2016, Part 4 Fire and Life Safety (pages 12-53 of Part 4).
_meta.purpose: Closes D25 (construction-type classification) and supplies the NBC half of D23 (required fire-resistance rating by structural element and construction type, plus occupancy applicability gates). Pairs with IS 456 Fig 1 / Table 16A (member dimension + cover for a given rating) already in file_for_DBR.json, which supply the *achieved* rating that NBC Table 1 sets the *required* target for.
_meta.extraction_note: This NBC PDF was supplied as clean, machine-readable text (NOT the scrambled-BIS-font case). Occupancy groups, construction types, and Table 1 are transcribed directly and need no verify flag. The only assembled (not single-table) item is the occupancy -> construction-type linkage, which NBC builds indirectly via Fire Zone; that derivation is documented in NBC_3.2_zone_to_type.
_meta.edition_anchor_correction: The prior methodology cited 'Table 2, with Clauses 3 & 4' for construction-type. In THIS edition the construction-type-defining table is Table 1 (Fire Resistance Ratings of Structural and Non-Structural Elements) under Clauses 3.3.1/3.3.2; Table 2 is the FAR table. Citations updated accordingly.
open_gaps: NBC gives required rating by construction TYPE (Table 1), not directly by occupancy group. Occupancy -> minimum required Type is assembled via Fire Zone (3.2.2.2 + 3.2.6).
Occupancy -> max permissible height limits live in the NOTES to Table 7 (firefighting installations), captured here as NBC_height_limits_by_occupancy.

clauses.title: Classification of Buildings Based on Occupancy - General
clauses.statement: All buildings, existing or hereafter erected, shall be classified by use/character of occupancy into one of nine groups: A Residential, B Educational, C Institutional, D Assembly, E Business, F Mercantile, G Industrial, H Storage, J Hazardous. A minor occupancy incidental to a main occupancy is classified under the main occupancy (3.1.1.1). Where two or more occupancies intermingle in one building it is treated as mixed occupancy (3.1.1.2 / 3.1.11) and governed by the most restrictive provisions applicable.

clauses.title: Occupancy Groups and Subdivisions (A-J)
clauses.statement: Full occupancy classification used as the input key for D25 and for the applicability gates of D23.

subdivisions.A-1: Lodging and rooming houses (inns, clubs, motels, guest houses)
subdivisions.A-2: One or two family private dwellings (sleeping accommodation up to 20 persons)
subdivisions.A-4: Apartment houses (three or more families, independent cooking; apartments, mansions, chawls)
subdivisions.A-5: Hotels up to Four Star category

subdivisions.B-1: Schools up to senior secondary level (>=20 students)

subdivisions.C-2: Custodial institutions (homes for aged/infirm, convalescent, orphanages)
subdivisions.C-3: Penal and mental institutions (jails, prisons, mental hospitals, reformatories)

subdivisions.D-1: Stage + fixed seats for over 1000 persons
subdivisions.D-2: Stage + fixed seats up to 1000 persons
subdivisions.D-3: No permanent stage, accommodation for 300 or more, no permanent seating
subdivisions.D-4: No permanent stage, accommodation for less than 300, no permanent seating
subdivisions.D-5: All other (incl. temporary) at ground level: grandstands, stadia, amusement parks, tents
subdivisions.D-6: Mixed assembly + mercantile (shopping malls with cinema/multiplex/food courts)
subdivisions.D-7: Underground and elevated mass rapid transit system

subdivisions.E-1: Offices, banks, professional establishments, post offices, police stations
subdivisions.E-2: Laboratories, outpatient clinics, research establishments, libraries, test houses
subdivisions.E-3: EDP centres, computer installations, IT parks, call centres
subdivisions.E-5: Broadcasting/TV stations, air traffic control towers

subdivisions.F-1: Shops/stores/markets, covered area up to 500 m2
subdivisions.F-2: Shops/stores/markets, covered area more than 500 m2

subdivisions.H: Storage/sheltering of goods, vehicles, animals (warehouses, cold storage, depots, garages, hangars, grain elevators)

subdivisions.J: Storage/handling/manufacture/processing of highly combustible or explosive materials, toxic/corrosive chemicals, explosive dusts

clauses.title: Types of Construction (Type 1 to Type 4)
clauses.statement: Fire resistance of a building/element is expressed in minutes against a specified fire load (kcal/m2) and fire intensity. For the Code, types of construction by fire resistance are classified into four categories: Type 1, Type 2, Type 3 and Type 4. The minimum fire-resistance ratings of structural and non-structural members for each type are given in Table 1. Non-combustible materials should be used; internal walls of staircase enclosures should be brickwork/RC/other with minimum 120 min rating.

clauses.title: Fire Resistance Rating Requirement
clauses.statement: Each structural and/or non-structural element shall have the requisite fire-resistance rating per Table 1, validated and certified. In the absence of a validated/certified rating, guidance may be taken from Annex C. Load-bearing steel beams/columns of buildings with total covered area 500 m2 and above shall be protected against failure/collapse in fire (Annex C Tables 25/26).

clauses.title: Fire Resistance Ratings of Structural and Non-Structural Elements (minutes)
notes: 1 The ratings are required to achieve the respective type of construction unless otherwise specified in the respective clauses for different applications/use.
2 In case of lift bank, the partition wall (if any) need not be of the fire rating specified in this table.

clauses.title: Occupancy -> Fire Zone -> Permitted Construction Type (assembled linkage)
clauses.statement: NBC does not give a single occupancy->construction-type table. It is assembled: occupancy maps to a Fire Zone (3.2.2.2), and each Fire Zone permits certain construction types for new buildings (3.2.6).

items.Fire_Zone_1: Group A (residential), B (educational), C (institutional), D (assembly), small business E-1, and F (mercantile)
items.Fire_Zone_2: Business E-2 to E-5, and industrial G-1 and G-2 (except high-hazard G-3)
items.Fire_Zone_3: High-hazard industrial G-3, storage H, and hazardous J

clauses.title: Permitted construction types by Fire Zone (new buildings)
Maximum permissible building height by occupancy (applicability gate)
clauses.statement: Occupancy-wise height ceilings drawn from the Notes to Table 7. Useful as a D23/D25 applicability gate and a sanity check on the DBR's stated occupancy vs height.

items.H_J: Not permitted above 15 m; MLCP not permitted above 45 m (Note 20)

clauses.title: Comparative Floor Area Ratios for Occupancies (One public street >= 9 m wide)
clauses.note: Included for disambiguation only (the methodology's 'Table 2' reference). This is the FAR table, NOT the fire-resistance table. UL = Unlimited, NP = Not Permitted.
